message.body: I'll start by setting up the worktree config and examining the census data for my owned integrations.
The census file isn't in this worktree. I'll work directly by grepping the owned integrations. Let me first understand the agnostic exception surface.
Now let me survey the requests references across my 14 owned integrations.
Most references are config/docs noise. Let me identify the real production catch sites. Note airflow has no production requests reference. Let me read the actual files that need changes.
Now I have the full production picture. Let me make the production edits.
Now consul, couch, couchbase:
Production edits done. Let me verify no requests references remain in production and that files compile.
Production compiles. Now let me examine the interception-path tests for each integration.
Let me examine each test file to distinguish harness (exempt) from interception-path.

These are all harness (fixture seeding, WaitFor, external GitHub, integration setup) - exempt. The interception-path tests are couch/test_couchv2, druid/test_unit, ecs_fargate/test_unit*, elastic/test_unit. Let me examine them.
Now elastic's test_unit.py:
Let me verify couch/test_couchv2.py is a harness/integration test (real server), not interception.
Now let me run the affected unit tests. Starting with the ones whose tests I modified.
The agnostic exceptions require a `message` arg. The parametrize passes classes, which mock instantiates with no args. I'll pass instances instead.
Druid passes. Now ecs_fargate and elastic:

Your run timed out (operational, not a code error) — your partial edits persist in this worktree. Continue from where you left off; do NOT restart from scratch. Three efficiency musts so you finish within the budget:

1) Run UNIT tests only: `ddev test <integration> -- -m unit`. Do NOT run the full `ddev test <integration>` — it attempts integration/e2e/docker envs and is far too slow across a batch. Only test integrations you actually modified.
2) Agnostic exceptions require a `message` positional arg. When using mock `side_effect`, pass INSTANCES, not classes: `side_effect=HTTPConnectionError('boom')`, never `side_effect=HTTPConnectionError`. Same for HTTPStatusError/HTTPTimeoutError/etc.
3) Many of your assigned integrations are ALREADY agnosticized in the base (from PR #24310). Run a quick grep first (`rg -n "requests" <int>/datadog_checks/`) and SKIP already-clean ones — spend time only on integrations with remaining `requests` refs. The committed gate `.r2/validate_slice_a.py` is your source of truth for "done".

Commit progress incrementally so nothing is lost if the run ends.
Production is clean (remaining matches are metric-name strings). Let me finish the elastic unit test run.
Elastic passes. Now let me run unit tests for the other integrations I modified production code in.
Several have no `unit`-marked tests. Let me check their markers and whether tests need docker.
I'll start by orienting myself and checking which of my owned integrations still have `requests` references.
Let me look at the agnostic exception surface and the base wrapper.
Now let me look at each production file that needs changes.
Now I understand all the changes. Let me make the production edits.
Now let me handle cloud_foundry_api dual-arm collapse.
Now verify all production packages are clean of requests imports/attrs.
Production is clean. Now let me examine the tests for interception-path `requests` references.

Both remaining test refs are harness HTTP (consul `WaitFor` ping, airflow upstream-docs metric freshness check) which are exempt. Now let me check tests for interception-path patterns: exception mocks, Session patches, Response construction.
Tests are largely already on the agnostic surface. Both remaining raw `requests` test refs are exempt harness HTTP. Let me set up ddev and run the unit tests.
Some integrations have no `-m unit` markers. Let me check their test structure.
These use unmarked tests, not `-m unit`. Let me run the non-docker tests (exclude integration/e2e).
All passing. Now let me run lint on the changed integrations.
All lint passes. Now let me add changelog entries for the 6 integrations with production changes (airflow had no shipped-code change).
Some changelog.d dirs don't exist yet. Let me create them.
arangodb and cloud_foundry_api already had 22676.fixed entries. Let me check what I overwrote.
These pre-existing entries (in HEAD) have better descriptive messages. Let me restore them and improve my new entries to match the established style.
Let me do a final verification pass across all 7 integrations.

All changes complete and verified. Here is my completion summary.

## Completion summary

### Scope
Batch A1 of the Phase 3 requests→agnostic migration, covering 7 integrations: **activemq_xml, airflow, ambari, arangodb, citrix_hypervisor, cloud_foundry_api, consul**.

### Production changes (6 files)
All narrowed to `datadog_checks.base.utils.http_exceptions.*` only; removed all `requests` imports/attribute use.

- **activemq_xml/datadog_checks/activemq_xml/activemq_xml.py**: dropped `import requests`; added `from datadog_checks.base.utils.http_exceptions import HTTPConnectionError`; `except requests.exceptions.ConnectionError:` → `except HTTPConnectionError:`.
- **ambari/datadog_checks/ambari/ambari.py**: `from requests.exceptions import ConnectionError, HTTPError, Timeout` → `from datadog_checks.base.utils.http_exceptions import HTTPConnectionError, HTTPStatusError, HTTPTimeoutError` (placed in the datadog_checks import group); `except (HTTPError, ConnectionError)` → `except (HTTPStatusError, HTTPConnectionError)`; `except Timeout:` → `except HTTPTimeoutError:`.
- **arangodb/datadog_checks/arangodb/check.py**: removed `from requests import HTTPError`; collapsed dual-catch `except (HTTPError, HTTPStatusError):` → `except HTTPStatusError:` (already imported `HTTPStatusError`).
- **citrix_hypervisor/datadog_checks/citrix_hypervisor/check.py**: removed `from datadog_checks.base.utils.http import requests`; `except (requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError, StdJSONDecodeError):` → `except StdJSONDecodeError:`. This is correct because the base `ResponseWrapper.json()` re-raises `requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError` as the stdlib `json.JSONDecodeError` (verified in `http.py` lines ~306-311), which `StdJSONDecodeError` (`from json import JSONDecodeError`) already catches — so the raw requests type can no longer surface.
- **cloud_foundry_api/datadog_checks/cloud_foundry_api/cloud_foundry_api.py**: removed `from requests.exceptions import HTTPError as RequestsHTTPError` and `from requests.exceptions import RequestException`; collapsed the three dual-arms `except (RequestException, HTTPError):` → `except HTTPError:` and `except (RequestsHTTPError, HTTPStatusError):` → `except HTTPStatusError:` (agnostic `HTTPError`/`HTTPStatusError` already imported).
- **consul/datadog_checks/consul/consul.py**: removed `import requests` and `from requests import HTTPError`; added `HTTPStatusError` to the existing `http_exceptions` import; `except (requests.exceptions.Timeout, HTTPTimeoutError)` → `except HTTPTimeoutError`; `except HTTPError as e:` → `except HTTPStatusError as e:` (the `e.response.status_code == 404` branch still works since agnostic `HTTPError` carries `.response`).

**airflow**: no production change needed. Its `datadog_checks/` package was already clean (the only matches were config-option names `log_requests` and conf.yaml.example comments, not requests-lib references); a prior commit under this same PR already migrated it (its `changelog.d/22676.fixed` predates my run).

### Tests
The interception-path unit tests for these integrations were already on the agnostic surface (from #24310 / prior commits): they use `mock_http`, `MockHTTPResponse`, and `http_exceptions` types, and pass exception **instances** as `side_effect` (e.g. `HTTPError("error")`). No test edits were required. The only remaining raw `requests` references in the test trees are exempt harness HTTP:
- `consul/tests/conftest.py`: `requests.get` inside `ping_cluster`, a `WaitFor` condition for `docker_run`.
- `airflow/tests/test_check_metrics_up_to_date.py`: `requests.get` fetching upstream Airflow docs to compare the metric list — a dev-only freshness check hitting GitHub, not the integration's HTTP path.

### Changelog entries
Created `22676.fixed` for the 6 integrations I changed. arangodb and cloud_foundry_api already had descriptive entries in HEAD (I initially overwrote them, then restored the originals via `git checkout`). Wrote new descriptive entries for activemq_xml, ambari, citrix_hypervisor, consul. airflow's entry already existed and was left untouched.

### Verification
- All 6 production files compile (`py_compile`).
- Agnostic gate: `rg` for `import requests|from requests|requests.exceptions|requests.Session|requests.Response|RequestException` across all 7 `datadog_checks/` packages returns empty; no `(RequestException, HTTPError)` dual-catch remains.
- Non-docker unit tests pass: ambari 16 passed/1 skipped, arangodb 4 passed, citrix_hypervisor 26 passed, cloud_foundry_api 23 passed, consul 25 passed/2 skipped. (activemq_xml has only `@pytest.mark.integration`/`e2e` tests — no mock-based unit test exists to cover its change without Docker, so it is verified by compile + agnostic gate only.)
- `ddev test --lint` passes for all 6 integrations (ruff format + check).
- `ddev config override` was applied; `ddev config show` confirms `core` points at this worktree.

### Things I noticed (not acted on, out of scope)
- **arangodb and cloud_foundry_api production files were still on the old dual-catch code in HEAD even though their `22676.fixed` changelog entries (describing the agnostic catch) were already committed.** My edits completed what those entries described. If other batches follow the same pattern, changelog entries may have been pre-committed ahead of the production narrowing.
- **citrix_hypervisor's fix depends on base behavior**: the correctness of dropping the `requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError` arm relies on `ResponseWrapper.json()` translating to stdlib `json.JSONDecodeError`. If that base seam ever changes, citrix's catch would need revisiting.
- **activemq_xml has no unit-test coverage for its exception path** — only integration/e2e tests (Docker-gated). Its production change is unverified by an executable test in this environment.
- Pydantic `PydanticDeprecatedSince212` warnings surface in config_models for arangodb, citrix_hypervisor, cloud_foundry_api (pre-existing, unrelated to this work).

for ALL changelog entries, revise them so that they are end user/customer-facing. for httpx-4 specifically, what changes is basically using agnostic exceptions. this is regardless of the bigger picture migration
The review asks me to make changelog entries end-user/customer-facing, focused on the agnostic-exceptions change rather than the migration narrative. Let me revise all 6 entries I own.
Now let me verify the validation gate passes.
The gate passes. All 6 changelog entries are now end-user-facing, describing the agnostic-exceptions change without the migration narrative.

Continued from the persisted partial edits (did not restart). All 6 production files were already narrowed to http_exceptions.* and compile; the agnostic gate .r2/validate_slice_a.py passes ("7 integrations clean"). Tests were already on the agnostic surface (mock_http/MockHTTPResponse, exception instances not classes), and non-docker unit tests passed for the modified integrations. This follow-up only revised the changelog entries per the review; no further code changes were needed.

Done. 

Summary of this follow-up:
- Rewrote all 6 changelog entries (activemq_xml, ambari, arangodb, citrix_hypervisor, cloud_foundry_api, consul) to a single end-user-facing line: "Use library-agnostic HTTP exceptions for error handling." — removing internal exception class names and the httpx-migration framing per the review.
- Verified the committed gate `.r2/validate_slice_a.py` passes ("7 integrations clean of requests-specific refs in production").
- No production or test code changes were needed in this pass; those were already complete and validated in the prior run.
